title: VPN on Linux: WireGuard, NordVPN, and ProtonVPN CLI Setup on Ubuntu and Debian
description: A command-line guide to setting up a VPN on Ubuntu and Debian. Covers WireGuard via wg-quick, NordVPN's official Linux CLI app, and ProtonVPN's Linux client, with actual package names and commands.
content:
## Three Ways to Run a VPN on Ubuntu/Debian

Linux gives you more VPN options than any other platform, and more ways to break things. Before you start: decide which approach fits your needs.

1. **WireGuard via wg-quick** -- manual config files, no VPN provider app, works with any WireGuard-compatible provider. Best for users who want minimal software and maximum control.
2. **NordVPN Linux CLI** -- official app from NordVPN, supports NordLynx (WireGuard-based), kill switch, DNS leak protection, split tunneling. Best for NordVPN subscribers who want features without manual config.
3. **ProtonVPN Linux CLI** -- official app from Proton, supports WireGuard natively, open source, supports NetShield (DNS-based ad/malware blocking). Best for ProtonVPN subscribers or privacy-focused users.

## Method 1: WireGuard via wg-quick

### Install WireGuard Tools

On Ubuntu 22.04, 24.04, or Debian 11/12:

```bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt install wireguard wireguard-tools
```

The `wireguard` package installs the kernel module. The `wireguard-tools` package provides `wg` and `wg-quick`.

Verify the kernel module loaded: `lsmod | grep wireguard`. If nothing returns, run `sudo modprobe wireguard`.

### Get a Config File

From **Mullvad:** go to account.mullvad.net/wireguard-config, generate a config for your chosen server, download the `.conf` file.

From **NordVPN:** log into your account, go to Manual Setup > WireGuard, generate a private key, and use the config generator for your chosen server.

From **ProtonVPN:** account.protonvpn.com > Downloads > WireGuard configuration, select a server, download.

### Place the Config and Connect

```bash
# Place the config (name it anything, e.g., wg-mullvad-nl1)
sudo cp ~/Downloads/mullvad-nl1.conf /etc/wireguard/wg0.conf
sudo chmod 600 /etc/wireguard/wg0.conf

# Bring up the tunnel
sudo wg-quick up wg0

# Check it is working
wg show
curl https://am.i.mullvad.net/connected
```

To connect at boot:

```bash
sudo systemctl enable wg-quick@wg0
sudo systemctl start wg-quick@wg0
```

To disconnect: `sudo wg-quick down wg0`.

### DNS Leak Prevention

By default, wg-quick uses the DNS server specified in the config's `DNS =` line and writes it to `/etc/resolv.conf`. On Ubuntu with systemd-resolved, this can leak DNS queries. To fix:

```bash
# Check if systemd-resolved is running
systemctl status systemd-resolved

# If it is, add to /etc/systemd/resolved.conf:
# DNSStubListener=no
# Then restart: sudo systemctl restart systemd-resolved
```

Alternatively, use the `resolvconf` package: `sudo apt install resolvconf`. The wg-quick `DNS` directive works correctly with resolvconf without extra steps.

## Method 2: NordVPN Linux CLI

NordVPN's Linux app is a proper CLI client with daemon-based management. It handles WireGuard (NordLynx), kill switch, and split tunneling without any manual config files.

### Install

```bash
curl https://downloads.nordcdn.com/apps/linux/install.sh | sh
```

Or, for a manual .deb install:

```bash
wget -qO- https://repo.nordvpn.com/deb/nordvpn/debian/pool/main/n/nordvpn/
# Find the latest version from the repo listing, then:
apt install ./nordvpn_<version>_amd64.deb
```

The package installs the `nordvpn` binary and the `nordvpnd` daemon. Add your user to the nordvpn group:

```bash
sudo usermod -aG nordvpn $USER
# Log out and back in for group membership to take effect
```

### Configure and Connect

```bash
# Log in (opens a browser URL, paste the callback URL back)
nordvpn login

# Set protocol to NordLynx (WireGuard-based)
nordvpn set technology nordlynx

# Enable kill switch (blocks traffic if VPN drops)
nordvpn set killswitch on

# Enable DNS leak protection
nordvpn set dns on

# Connect to fastest server
nordvpn connect

# Connect to a specific country
nordvpn connect Netherlands

# Connect to a specific city
nordvpn connect Amsterdam

# Check status
nordvpn status

# List available countries
nordvpn countries
```

### Split Tunneling

```bash
# Enable split tunneling
nordvpn set splitTunneling on

# Exclude an app from the VPN tunnel
nordvpn whitelist add process transmission-gtk

# Exclude a subnet
nordvpn whitelist add subnet 192.168.1.0/24
```

The NordVPN Linux app (v4.5.0, released March 2026) added post-quantum encryption support. Enable it: `nordvpn set post-quantum on` (NordLynx only).

## Method 3: ProtonVPN Linux CLI

ProtonVPN's Linux client is open source (MIT license, available on GitHub at protonvpn/proton-vpn-gtk-app). The CLI-only version installs the daemon without the GUI.

### Install on Ubuntu/Debian

```bash
# Add the ProtonVPN repo
wget -q -O - https://repo.protonvpn.com/debian/public_key.asc | gpg --dearmor | sudo tee /etc/apt/trusted.gpg.d/proton.gpg
echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture)] https://repo.protonvpn.com/debian testing main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/proton.list
sudo apt update

# Install the CLI (no GUI)
sudo apt install proton-vpn-gnome-desktop
# Or for CLI only:
sudo apt install python3-proton-vpn-api-core protonvpn-cli
```

### Connect

```bash
protonvpn-cli login <your_protonvpn_email>

# Connect to fastest server
protonvpn-cli connect --fastest

# Connect to a specific country (NL = Netherlands)
protonvpn-cli connect --cc NL

# Connect to a specific server
protonvpn-cli connect NL#3

# Connect using WireGuard explicitly
protonvpn-cli connect --protocol wireguard --fastest

# Check status
protonvpn-cli status

# Disconnect
protonvpn-cli disconnect
```

ProtonVPN's Linux client always uses WireGuard as the transport when available. The `protonvpn-cli` package handles key generation, server selection, DNS, and routing automatically.

### Enable Kill Switch

```bash
protonvpn-cli killswitch --on
```

This adds iptables rules that block all non-VPN traffic. The kill switch persists across reconnects.

## Checking for DNS and IP Leaks

After connecting with any method:

```bash
# Check your public IP
curl https://ipinfo.io/ip

# Check DNS resolution
curl https://ipinfo.io/json

# Detailed leak check
curl https://am.i.mullvad.net/json
```

## Recommendation

For most Ubuntu/Debian users, the **NordVPN Linux CLI** offers the best combination of ease and features. The kill switch, split tunneling, and automatic server selection work without any manual config file management, and NordLynx (WireGuard) performance matches raw WireGuard speeds. [NordVPN](https://nordvpn.com) supports Debian 11+, Ubuntu 20.04+, and most popular derivatives including Linux Mint and Pop!_OS. If you prefer open-source software throughout your stack, ProtonVPN's client is the privacy-respecting alternative.
